senses.definition: producing a thin, metallic, hollow sound or of poor quality, especially regarding audio or material; sounding cheap or tinny
examples: The radio crackled and the speaker sounded tinny.
The drum had a tinny resonance when struck.

senses.definition: a tinny sound or quality; something that sounds cheap or thin
examples: There was a tinny in the recording that distracted listeners.
The tinny of the bell grated on nerves.

senses.definition: a term of endearment or nickname; also a family or personal name element in some languages
examples: He called his son Tino as a nickname.
Tino arrived early for the meeting.

senses.definition: an extinct genus of nautiloid molluscs with a conical shell
examples: Paleontologists found a tinoceras fossil in the formation.
The tinoceras shell shows ribbing patterns.

senses.definition: a member of the family Tinoceratidae, an extinct group of cephalopods
examples: Researchers compared the tinoceratid features to other nautiloids.
The tinoceratid lineage spans several geological periods.

senses.definition: a taxonomic genus name in biology; often used in scientific contexts
examples: The tinosa species exhibits unique morphological features.
Taxonomic keys distinguish tinosa from related genera.

senses.definition: thin sheet of metal, typically tin-coated steel, used for cans and packaging
examples: The factory produces food tins from tinplate.
They bought a sheet of tinplate to repair the bicycle bell.

senses.definition: to coat or plate with tin
examples: They tinplate the new cans to resist corrosion.
Companies tinplate steel goods for storage use.

senses.definition: coated with tin; covered in a thin metal layer that looks dull or cheap
examples: The tinplated cans corroded quickly.
The old tool had a tinplated surface.

senses.definition: plural of tinplate; thin sheets of tin-coated steel used for packaging
examples: The warehouse stored thousands of tinplates.
Markets for tinplates declined with alternative packaging.

senses.definition: the process of coating something with a thin layer of tin
examples: Tinplating protects steel from corrosion.
They discussed tinplating as a cost-saving method.

senses.definition: of poor quality or shoddy; petty or inferior in character; slangy meaning lacking taste or quality
examples: The tinpot dictator model is dismissed by analysts.
That tinpot argument won’t persuade anyone.

senses.definition: plural of tinpot; people in power regarded as petty or ineffective
examples: The tinpots refused to listen to experts.
Citizens mocked the tinpots in charge.

senses.definition: metal containers made of tin or tin-coated metal; cans
examples: I opened a tin to get some soup.
The cookies are stored in a tin.

senses.definition: to cover or store in tins; to store food in metal containers
examples: They tin the preserves and label them.
She tins the jams for the winter.

senses.definition: thin metallic foil used for decoration, especially at Christmas
examples: The tree was draped with gold tinsel.
She hung tinsel on the mantlepiece.

senses.definition: to ornament with or use tinsel
examples: They tinsel the tree every year.
The hall was tinselled for the party.

senses.definition: decorated with tinsel
examples: The tinseled branches glowed in the light.
A tinseled Christmas tree stood in the corner.

senses.definition: the act of decorating with tinsel; decorative process
examples: Tinseling finished just before guests arrived.
The tinseling of the hall took longer than expected.

senses.definition: decorated with tin or tin-like material; adorned with tinsel
examples: A tinselled hat shimmered under the lights.
The tinselled curtains added festive sparkle.

senses.definition: like tin; resembling tin in appearance or quality
examples: The metal had tinsellike sheen.
An object with tinsellike durability.

senses.definition: the act of selling tin or tin-plated products; can be used metaphorically
examples: The tinselling of cans declined with plastics.
Regional tinselling markets expanded.

senses.definition: showy but of cheap quality; flashy in a tasteless way
examples: The tinselly decorations looked gaudy.
Her tinselly outfit drew mixed reactions.

senses.definition: a person or company that makes tinsel
examples: The tinselmaker supplied decorative strands for the festival.
Historically, a tinselmaker produced reflective garland.

senses.definition: the craft or industry of making tinsel
examples: Tinselmaking has declined with modern decorative plastics.
He studied tinselmaking in art school.

senses.definition: decorative tinsel ornaments or trimmings; garlands
examples: The hall was adorned with various tinselries.
Tinselries hung along the staircase.

senses.definition: the craft of making or selling tinsel; decorative work using tin
examples: Her tinselry impressed festival organizers.
Tinselry required careful workmanship.

senses.definition: plural of tinsel; decorative strips or threads
examples: The dress was trimmed with delicate tinsels.
They wrapped gifts with tinsels.

senses.definition: a person who weaves or creates tinsel decorations
examples: The tinselweaver crafted sparkling garlands.
A skilled tinselweaver worked for the festival.
